name: workers
context: Deploy and manage Cloudflare Workers — JavaScript/TypeScript edge functions running in V8 isolates
info: Cloudflare Workers are serverless functions that run at the edge in V8 isolates across 300+ data centers globally. Unlike container-based serverless, Workers have sub-millisecond cold starts and execute JavaScript, TypeScript, or WebAssembly. Workers use the Web Standards API (fetch, Request, Response, Headers, URL, crypto, streams) — not Node.js APIs. The entry point is a module that exports a `fetch` handler. Workers connect to storage services (KV, R2, D1, Queues) through bindings configured in `wrangler.toml`. Deploy with `wrangler deploy`. Agents use this skill to deploy API endpoints, proxies, middleware, scheduled tasks, and edge applications.
triggers: set up a cloudflare worker project

sections.title: Quick start
sections.body: Run `npm create cloudflare@latest my-worker` to scaffold a new project. This creates `wrangler.toml`, `src/index.ts`, and `package.json`. Run `wrangler dev` for local development and `wrangler deploy` to push to production. See [[cloudflare/auth]] for authentication setup before deploying.

sections.title: Module syntax entry point
sections.body: Workers use ES module syntax. Export a default object with handler methods: `fetch(request, env, ctx)` for HTTP, `scheduled(event, env, ctx)` for cron, `queue(batch, env, ctx)` for Queues. The `env` parameter contains all bindings and environment variables. The `ctx` parameter provides `waitUntil()` and `passThroughOnException()`.

sections.title: Deploying Workers
sections.body: Run `wrangler deploy` to push the Worker to production. Use `--env staging` for named environments. Workers are deployed globally to all 300+ Cloudflare data centers within seconds. Use `wrangler tail` to stream live logs after deployment. Roll back with `wrangler rollback` or by redeploying a previous version.

sections.title: Environment variables and secrets
sections.body: Plain text variables go in `[vars]` in `wrangler.toml`. Secrets are set with `wrangler secret put <NAME>` and are encrypted at rest. Both are accessed via `env.NAME` in the Worker. Secrets are never shown in the dashboard or API after creation. Use secrets for API keys, database credentials, and tokens.

sections.title: Cron triggers
sections.body: Add `[triggers]` with `crons` array in `wrangler.toml` to schedule Worker execution. The `scheduled` handler receives `event.cron` (the matching pattern) and `event.scheduledTime`. Use `ctx.waitUntil()` for async work. Cron Workers don't receive HTTP requests — they run on a schedule.

sections.title: Bindings overview
sections.body: Bindings connect Workers to Cloudflare services with zero-latency references. Configure in `wrangler.toml`: `[[kv_namespaces]]` for KV, `[[r2_buckets]]` for R2, `[[d1_databases]]` for D1, `[[queues.producers]]` for Queues. Access via `env.BINDING_NAME` in the Worker. See [[cloudflare/workers/bindings]] for detailed configuration.
